messages.content: what is meant by the term coordination ? short sentence
Coordination refers to the process of organizing and harmonizing different elements or activities to achieve a common goal.
why coordination is necesseray? short sentence
Coordination is necessary to ensure efficient and effective execution of tasks, avoid duplication of efforts, and promote cooperation among individuals or teams.

Economic principle
• Utility orientation
• Efficiency versus
effectiveness ? i dont understand this

In economics, utility orientation refers to individuals' preferences and decisions being driven by their desire to maximize their satisfaction or utility from consuming goods and services. 

Efficiency, in this context, refers to achieving the desired output with minimal waste or resources, while effectiveness means the ability to successfully achieve a desired outcome or goal. In other words, efficiency focuses on doing things right, while effectiveness focuses on doing the right things.
The principle of the division of labour short sentence
The principle of the division of labour involves breaking down tasks into specialized and repetitive activities to increase productivity and efficiency.
The principle of the division of labour short sentence i dont undersatnd it

The principle of the division of labour suggests that when tasks are divided and assigned to specific individuals or groups, it can lead to better productivity and efficiency within a system or organization.
The Principle of Ownership – Property-rights short sentence
The principle of ownership, also known as property rights, refers to the legal and moral rights individuals have to possess, control, and use property or resources.
The Principle of Ownership – Property-rights short simple  sentence very simple
Property rights are the rights individuals have to own and control property or resources.

Use the
good
Earn
income
from it
Change
it
Transfer
it
The principle of ownership or property rights allows individuals to use, earn income from, change, and transfer goods or resources they own.
Property rights – Quintessences i dont  understand it short sentence
Property rights are fundamental legal rights that allow individuals to own, control, and benefit from their property or resources.
Acting units of economics the goal in a short sentences
The goal of acting units in economics is to maximize their utility or satisfaction through rational decision-making and efficient allocation of resources.
Goods Market - Term short sentence
The goods market refers to the marketplace where goods and services are bought and sold between buyers and sellers.
Perfect market ... in a short sentence
A perfect market refers to an idealized market structure where there is perfect competition, complete information, and no barriers to entry or exit.
Competition in the market in short sentence
Competition in the market refers to the rivalry among sellers in their efforts to gain market share, attract customers, and achieve higher sales and profits.
Competition in the market in a short sentences
Competition in the market is the presence of multiple sellers competing against each other to offer products or services to consumers.
Traded goods short sentences
Traded goods are products or commodities that are bought and sold between different countries or regions in international trade.
Transaction Cost Theory short sentence

Transaction cost theory suggests that the cost of conducting economic transactions, such as searching for information, bargaining, and enforcing agreements, influences the structure and behavior of organizations and markets.

Why do we divide the work and how do
we protect property rights?? short sentence

We divide work to increase productivity and efficiency. Property rights are protected through legal frameworks and enforcement mechanisms to ensure individuals' ownership and control over their property.

How can a good business model make
companies successful? short sentence
A good business model can make companies successful by identifying and exploiting profitable opportunities, creating value for customers, and establishing a sustainable revenue-generating framework.
How can you develop a business model? short sentence

A business model can be developed by analyzing market trends, identifying target customers, creating a value proposition, determining revenue streams, and assessing key resources and partnerships required for success.

What do companies have to consider
about location and legal form?  short sentence

Companies need to consider factors such as proximity to target markets, availability of resources, transportation infrastructure, and legal requirements when deciding on the location and legal form for their business.

Why is it not just "traded" or why are
there companies? short sentence

Companies exist because they provide a structured and organized framework for producing and delivering goods and services efficiently, offering economies of scale, specialized resources, and assuming risks that individuals alone cannot easily achieve in a traded system.

How do people influence each other in the
economy?? short sentence

People influence each other in the economy through their consumption patterns, production decisions, market behavior, and interactions, which collectively shape supply and demand dynamics and drive economic outcomes.
